---
title: "Overview"
---

## Goals:

- See AI in action: live code understanding, revision, and reuse with AI

- Setup: Positron + GitHub Copilot + Stata

- Understand how AI works and relevant vocabulary:

  - IDE, LLM, Assistant, tokens, prompts, context window, agent, etc.

- Get ready to practice on your own during the self-study exercises

- Familiarize with key principles and the workflow


## Agenda

**Part 1 — Setup & Live Demo (80 min)**

| Time   | Topic |
|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| 10 min | Welcome, introductions, and overview |
| 30 min | Setup: Positron + GitHub Copilot + Stata + Working folder |
| 40 min | Agentic coding with Stata/R: Example 1 |
| 10 min | ☕ Break (10 min) |
| 30 min | AI overview presentation: how GitHub Copilot and LLMs work, key concepts, and capabilities |
| 30 min | Cookbook: securing sensitive data, guardrails, and responsible AI use |
| 30 min | Self-study exercises overview and Q&A |

## Before You Arrive

Try completing the prerequisites:

1. [Software Setup](../prerequisites/software.qmd)
2. [GitHub Setup](../prerequisites/github.qmd)
3. [Positron Extensions](../prerequisites/extensions.qmd)
4. [Positron Assistant](../prerequisites/assistant.qmd)

If you run into issues, check [Common Problems](../prerequisites/common-problems.qmd) or
contact [ITHelp@worldbankgroup.org](mailto:ITHelp@worldbankgroup.org) before the session.

## Materials

### Welcome, introductions, and overview

```{=html}
<iframe class="slide-deck"  src="ai4coding-slides-d1-1/"></iframe>
```

View slides in a [new tab](ai4coding-slides-d1-1/){target="_blank"}.

### Setup overview

Important on folder structure:

::: {.callout-warning}
**Do not use OneDrive for developing code during this course! (temporary)**.

- Positron is a new software for the WB.
- Unfortunately we only discovered last week that WB security policies block Positron from writing files on OneDrive.
- This means that you will not be able to save your code or data if you are working in a OneDrive folder.
- New policy could only be implemented after the spring meeting freeze is over after Friday, April 24.

**Follow these instructions instead.**

1. On `C:\WBG\` create a folder called `ai`.

2. Copy the exercise templates from shared [One Drive WB only folder `ai4coding-examples`](https://worldbankgroup-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/ebukin_worldbank_org/IgCXQVakSxEpT7VJFmKp_qtnAXx8RX9iemnwfGwITkEA3bE?e=U08qTX) or GitHub [worldbank/ai4coding-examples](https://github.com/worldbank/ai4coding-examples)  `C:\WBG\ai\ai4coding-practice\`

3. Work on your local copies of exercises in `C:\WBG\ai\ai4coding-practice\ex0...\`.

4. Data shared [One Drive WB only `ai4coding-data`](https://worldbankgroup-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/personal/ebukin_worldbank_org/Documents/AI/AI-course-2026-April/ai4coding-data?csf=1&web=1&e=ITG6Sk) can still be used from the one-drive as it is read only.
   If there are any problems, copy the data to `C:\WBG\ai\ai4coding-data\` and use it from there.
:::

```{=html}
<iframe class="slide-deck" src="ai4coding-slides-d1-2/"></iframe>
```

View slides in a [new tab](ai4coding-slides-d1-2/){target="_blank"}.

## Exercise 1: Stata in Positron with GitHub Copilot (Not covered in the live session)

The goal of this exercise is to get familiar with Positron's AI assistance
features and how they can be used to write and execute Stata code. We go
through a typical workflow of creating a Stata do file, writing code with
AI assistance, and executing it in the console.

This exercise overviews the following features of Positron's AI assistance:

- inline suggestions: accepting, rejecting, and customizing
- chat mode: asking for code generation, revision, and troubleshooting
- inline chat: revising specific code snippets without leaving the editor
- agent mode: automating code execution and debugging
- context monitoring

Full exercise details, suggested prompts and instructions are in [ex1-1-explore-positron.qmd](ex1-1-explore-positron.qmd).

Pre-recorded video (18 min.) is here:

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/embed/U29xAoivY5Y >}}


## Exercise 2: Understanding and cleaning the data (Covered in the live session)

<!-- TODO -->

This Excersie inproduces principles of effective prompting for making AI
results more deterministic when working with the data. It also shows key methods
how confidential data can be protected when working with AI to minimize the risk of data exposure.

Full exercise details, suggested prompts, and instructions are in [Exercise 2: Understanding and cleaning the data with AI](ex1-2-understand-clean-data.qmd).

<!-- TODO: Pre-recorded video is here:

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/embed/QU0N--y-skA >}} -->

## Presentation: How AI works, key concepts, and capabilities

```{=html}
<iframe class="slide-deck" src="ai4coding-slides-d1-3/"></iframe>

```
View slides in a [new tab](ai4coding-slides-d1-3/){target="_blank"} or download a [PDF version](day1/ai4coding-slides-d1-3/ai4coding-slides-d1-3.pdf).

<!-- TODO: Additional materials on AI concepts and capabilities worth familiarizing with. A pills tab with links to videos and reading -->
